title: قواعد السلامة على الإنترنت للأطفال
text_original: Online Protection Creating a Establish a clear set of rules that are prominently placed and echo key safer and age- points from the terms of service and acceptable use guidelines. User- appropriate online friendly language for these rules should define: • the nature of the service and what is expected of its users; (cont.) • what is and is not acceptable in terms of content, behaviour and language, as well as prohibiting illegal usage; • the consequences proportionate to the breach, for example, reporting to law enforcement or suspension of the user’s account. Make it easy for customers to report concerns about misuse to customer care, with standard and accessible processes in place to deal with different concerns, such as receiving unwanted communications (e.g. spam SMS). Be transparent and provide customers with clear information about the nature of the services offered, for example: • type of content/service and costs; • minimum age required for access; • availability of parental controls, including what the controls cover (e.g. network) or do not cover (e.g. Wi-Fi) and training on how to use • type of user information collected and how it is used. Promote national support services that enable children and young people to report and seek support in the case of abuse or exploitation (see, for example, Child Helpline International). Educating Industry can complement technical measures with educational and children, parents empowerment activities by taking the following actions: about children’s Clearly describe available content and corresponding parental controls safety and their or
text_simplified: يجب على الأهل وضع قواعد واضحة ومكتوبة لاستخدام الإنترنت، مع شرح ما هو مسموح وما هو ممنوع، والعواقب المترتبة على المخالفات. كما ينبغي تعليم الأطفال كيفية الإبلاغ عن أي مشكلة أو محتوى غير لائق، والاستفادة من أدوات الرقابة الأبوية المتاحة. من المهم أيضًا تعريف الأطفال بخدمات الدعم الوطنية التي تساعدهم في حال التعرض للإساءة أو الاستغلال.
